questions.question: Explain the concept of decision parameter in the Midpoint Circle Algorithm and how it determines pixel selection.
keyPoints: Decision parameter pk tests the midpoint between candidate pixels
If pk < 0, midpoint is inside circle → choose East pixel
If pk ≥ 0, midpoint is outside/on circle → choose South-East pixel
pk is updated based on which pixel was selected

questions.question: Derive the initial value of the decision parameter p₀ for the Midpoint Circle Algorithm.
keyPoints: Starting point is (0, r)
Midpoint between (1, r-1) and (1, r) is (1, r-0.5)
Substitute in circle equation: 1² + (r-0.5)² - r² = 1 + r² - r + 0.25 - r² = 1 - r + 0.25 = 1.25 - r
For integer arithmetic, multiply by 2: 2.5 - 2r, round to get 1 - r
Therefore, p₀ = 1 - r

questions.question: How is 8-way symmetry exploited in the Midpoint Circle Algorithm? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Circle has 8-way symmetry due to reflection properties
Only need to calculate points for one octant (0° to 45°)
Once point (x, y) is found, reflect to get 7 other points: (x, y), (y, x), (y, -x), (x, -y), (-x, -y), (-y, -x), (-y, x), (-x, y)
This reduces computation by factor of 8

questions.question: State and explain the stopping condition for the Midpoint Circle Algorithm.
keyPoints: Algorithm stops when x > y (or x ≥ y depending on convention)
This is the 45° line where octants meet
At this point, we have covered the first octant completely
Further iteration would repeat points already generated

questions.question: Compare the Midpoint Circle Algorithm with trigonometric methods for circle drawing.
keyPoints: Trigonometric: Uses sin/cos functions, requires floating-point operations
Midpoint: Uses only integer arithmetic, faster
Trigonometric: Generates points at regular angular intervals
Midpoint: Generates points at equal x-intervals
Midpoint is more efficient for pixel-based displays

questions.question: Write the update formulas for the decision parameter when (a) pk < 0 and (b) pk ≥ 0.
keyPoints: For pk < 0 (East pixel chosen): pk+1 = pk + 2xk+1 + 1
For pk ≥ 0 (South-East pixel chosen): pk+1 = pk + 2xk+1 - 2yk+1 + 1
xk+1 = xk + 1 (x always increments)
yk+1 = yk (E) or yk - 1 (SE)

questions.question: Trace the Midpoint Circle Algorithm for r = 5. Show all intermediate values until the algorithm terminates.
keyPoints: Start at (0, 5), p₀ = 1 - 5 = -4
k=5: x=5, y=2, x > y, stop
Generate all 8-way symmetric points

questions.question: Explain the Midpoint Circle Algorithm in detail. Include initial decision parameter, pixel selection criteria, update formulas, and termination condition.
keyPoints: Algorithm draws circle using integer arithmetic
Start at (0, r), p₀ = 1 - r
For each step, calculate midpoint between E and SE
If p < 0: select E (x+1, y), update p = p + 2x + 1
If p ≥ 0: select SE (x+1, y-1), update p = p + 2x - 2y + 1
Stop when x > y
Use 8-way symmetry to plot all points
Complexities: O(r) time, O(1) space

questions.question: Explain why the Midpoint Circle Algorithm is preferred over trigonometric methods in computer graphics. Discuss the advantages and mention any limitations.
keyPoints: Trigonometric uses sin/cos which require floating-point operations
Midpoint uses only integer arithmetic - faster
Trigonometric: points at equal angular intervals, not pixel-equidistant
Midpoint: maintains uniform pixel distribution
Limitation: only approximates circle, not exact
Limited to integer radius in basic form
Works only for circles, not general curves

questions.question: Modify the Midpoint Circle Algorithm to draw a circle with center at (h, k) instead of origin. Write the pseudocode.
keyPoints: For each calculated point (x, y), add translation: (x+h, y+k)
Also reflect with translation: (y+h, x+k), (-y+h, x+k), etc.
Pseudocode: Initialize x = 0, y = r, p = 1-r
While x < y: plot (x+h, y+k) and 7 symmetric points
Update p and x, y accordingly
Continue until x ≥ y
